chapter_title: Products of animal origin, not elsewhere specified or included
scope_note: Note 1: Ch.05 does NOT cover: (a) edible products (EXCEPT guts/bladders/stomachs and animal blood liquid/dried); (b) hides/skins (except goods of 05.05 and parings/waste of 05.11) → Ch.41 or 43; (c) animal textile materials except horsehair/horsehair waste → Section XI; (d) prepared knots/tufts for brush-making → 96.03. Note 2: sorting human hair by length does NOT constitute 'working'. Note 3: elephant, hippo, walrus, narwhal, wild boar tusks; rhinoceros horns; ALL animal teeth = 'ivory' throughout the Nomenclature. Note 4: 'horsehair' = hair of manes or tails of equine OR bovine animals — 05.11 covers horsehair and horsehair waste whether or not put up as a layer with or without supporting material.
inclusions: Heading 05.01 — Human hair, unworked (whether or not washed/scoured); waste of human hair. Note 2: sorting by length is NOT 'working' — sorted hair still in Ch.05. Excludes wigs (67.03) and worked hair articles (67.04)
Heading 05.02 — Pigs', hogs' or boars' bristles/hair; badger hair and other brush-making hair; waste. For paint brushes, toothbrushes, shaving brushes, artist brushes

Heading 05.04 — Guts, bladders and stomachs of animals (other than fish), whole and pieces, fresh/chilled/frozen/salted/in brine/dried/smoked: natural sausage casings (sheep/hog/beef casings), cattle bladders, rumen/stomach for rennet extraction. PERMITTED exclusion from general Ch.02 edibility rule

Heading 05.05 — Skins and other parts of birds with their feathers or down; feathers/parts of feathers (quills may be trimmed) and down — not further worked than cleaned, disinfected or treated for preservation; powder and waste thereof: raw duck/goose down, raw decorative feathers, raw feather powder. Excludes dressed/prepared feathers → 67.01

Heading 05.06 — Bones and horn-cores, unworked, defatted, simply prepared (but NOT cut to shape), treated with acid or degelatinised; powder and waste: raw cattle/pig/poultry bones for gelatin/glue/bone meal production, horn-cores

Heading 05.07 — Ivory (Note 3: = ALL tusks/rhino horn/all animal teeth); tortoiseshell; whalebone and whalebone hair; horns; antlers; hooves; nails; claws; beaks — unworked or simply prepared but NOT cut to shape; powder and waste. Elephant/hippo/walrus/narwhal tusks → 05.07. ALL animal teeth → 05.07. Rhinoceros horns → 05.07

Heading 05.08 — Coral and similar materials, unworked or simply prepared but not otherwise worked; shells of molluscs/crustaceans/echinoderms and cuttlebone — unworked or simply prepared but not cut to shape; powder/waste: raw coral (red/white/pink), oyster shells, scallop shells, mother-of-pearl shells, cuttlebone (for pet birds)
Heading 05.09 — Natural sponges of animal origin: Mediterranean/Caribbean bath sponges (Spongia/Hippospongia species), cleaned/bleached natural sponges — still animal-origin material, not synthetic

Heading 05.11 — Animal products NES; dead animals of Ch.01 or Ch.03 unfit for human consumption: horsehair and horsehair waste (Note 4: mane/tail hair of equine OR bovine), whether or not in layers with/without supporting material; animal blood (inedible); parings/waste of raw hides; sinews/tendons; unborn calf skins; animal semen (AI straws); fish waste (inedible); embryos for transplantation

exclusions.condition: Edible products intended for human consumption — EXCEPT guts/bladders/stomachs (05.04) and animal blood (liquid/dried) which are specifically kept in Ch.05 by Note 1 exception
exclusions.action: Redirect to Ch.02 (meat/offal), Ch.03 (fish/seafood), Ch.04 (dairy/eggs/honey/insects). Note 1(a) makes a crucial exception: guts (05.04) and animal blood (05.11/30.02) remain in Ch.05 even though they may be edible in some cultures. Tripe (edible stomach lining prepared as food) → 02.06. Whole stomach as casing → 05.04.

exclusions.condition: Hides or skins of any animals (including furskins) — EXCEPT birdskins with feathers (05.05) and parings/waste of raw hides (05.11)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 41 (leather/raw hides without fur) or Chapter 43 (furskins tanned with hair on). Raw cattle/pig hide → Ch.41. Mink pelt → Ch.43. Sheepskin with wool (for leathermaking) → Ch.41. Remember: only BIRDSKINS with FEATHERS → 05.05. PARINGS/WASTE of raw hides → 05.11

exclusions.condition: Animal textile materials other than horsehair and horsehair waste
exclusions.action: Redirect to Section XI (textiles): wool → Ch.51. Cashmere/mohair → Ch.51. Raw silk/silk waste for spinning → Ch.50. HORSEHAIR specifically stays in Ch.05 (05.11) per Note 1(c) exception — horsehair is NOT a textile material in HS classification. Reminder: 'horsehair' = mane/tail hair of equine or bovine (Note 4)

exclusions.condition: Prepared knots or tufts of bristles for broom or brush making (ready-to-insert brush head bundles)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 96.03 — Note 1(d). RAW BRISTLES not yet prepared into brush bundles → 05.02. PREPARED KNOTS/TUFTS (cut to size, tied, cleaned, shaped for insertion into brush handles) → 96.03

classification_rules.rule: Note 3 — Universal 'ivory' definition: not just elephant — ALL specified tusks + ALL animal teeth
classification_rules.description: Note 3 defines 'ivory' THROUGHOUT THE NOMENCLATURE (not just Ch.05) as including: TUSKS of: elephant, hippopotamus, walrus, narwhal, wild boar. HORNS of: rhinoceros. TEETH of: ALL animals (all animal teeth = ivory in HS context — cow teeth, horse teeth, whale teeth, scrimshaw material). This broad definition means: shark teeth → 'ivory' → 05.07 unworked; 96.01 if worked. Sperm whale teeth → 'ivory'. Wild boar tusk → 'ivory' → 05.07. Worked ivory (carved) of any of these species → 96.01.

classification_rules.rule: Note 4 — 'Horsehair' includes bovine tail/mane hair, and covers layered assemblies
classification_rules.description: Note 4 defines 'horsehair' (used in heading 05.11) as hair of the MANES or TAILS of EQUINE OR BOVINE animals — this is critical: BOVINE (cattle) mane/tail hair = 'horsehair' for HS purposes. Heading 05.11 covers horsehair / horsehair waste whether or not PUT UP AS A LAYER WITH OR WITHOUT SUPPORTING MATERIAL (e.g., horsehair pad for upholstery — raw horsehair sandwiched between fabric) → still 05.11 while unprocessed. Once spun into yarn → 51.10. Woven → 51.13.

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — Sorting human hair by length does NOT constitute working (05.01 classification retained)
classification_rules.description: Heading 05.01 covers UNWORKED human hair. Note 2 clarifies that SORTING hair by length (provided root ends and tip ends are NOT arranged together — i.e., not 'drawn' or 'combed' for wig-making) shall NOT be deemed to constitute 'working'. Implications: Raw human hair simply sorted by length → still 05.01. Human hair sorted with roots and tips uniformly oriented (for wig-making preparation) = 'working' — borderline with 67.03. Rejected/short human hair clippings (waste) → 05.01.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 05.06/05.07/05.08 (unworked/simply prepared) vs 96.01 (worked articles) — the 'simply prepared' limit
classification_rules.description: Ch.05 materials in UNWORKED or SIMPLY PREPARED state only. 'Simply prepared' = cleaning, drying, bleaching, degreasing (bones), treating with acid (bones), degelatinising (bones), cutting to approximately rectangular blocks (ivory/horn). NOT 'simply prepared' → 96.01: cutting to final specific shapes, drilling for jewellery, polishing, engraving, artistic shaping. Examples: Raw elephant tusk → 05.07. Ivory slabs cut to rectangular blocks → 05.07. Carved ivory netsuke/figurine → 96.01. Coral cleaned and bleached → 05.08. Coral carved into beads → 96.01.

classification_rules.rule: 05.11 catch-all: animal semen, embryos, inedible blood, and functional assemblies (horsehair layers)
classification_rules.description: Heading 05.11 is the residual within Ch.05 covering miscellaneous: ANIMAL SEMEN (bull/boar/stallion AI straws — for artificial insemination) → 05.11. ANIMAL EMBRYOS (for transplantation) → 05.11. INEDIBLE ANIMAL BLOOD (not pharmaceutical) → 05.11. BLOOD for pharmaceutical use (plasma, antisera) → 30.02. SINEWS/TENDONS (industrial) → 05.11. PARINGS of raw hides → 05.11. FISH WASTE (inedible fins/viscera/scales for industrial use) → 05.11. DEAD ANIMALS (from Ch.01 or Ch.03) that are UNFIT for human consumption → 05.11. Note: live animal semen for import is NOT 05.11 — live animals → Ch.01. 05.11 requires the product to be separated from the animal.
